'''''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0''' was a supplemental comic to [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse's]] [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] comic series. It was included in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic/Rebellion flip-book]].

The idea of such a flip-book was suggested by [[John Jackson Miller]] when the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' series was already in production. As a result, [[Brian Ching]] was distracted from drawing the main series, and [[Travel Foreman]] drew the [[Knights of the Old Republic 5|fifth issue]] instead. Issue 0 was actually released after [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|issue 1]], and served as a prequel of sorts, but the issues were reordered chronologically for the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Volume 1: Commencement|trade paperback]].

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=CROSSROADS
|text=It is a time of upheaval for the galaxy. Nearly four
thousand years before the birth of Luke Skywalker, the
Mandalorian Wars have strained the OLD REPUBLIC's
resources to the breaking point.

With so many Republic forces at the front, systems
in the Outer Rim teeter on the edge of lawlessness—
even the city-world of TARIS, which was once a thriving
commercial center. Here, as elsewhere, the Republic
relies increasingly on JEDI KNIGHTS and their students
to help maintain order.

But some Knights, frustrated with the conduct of the
Wars, argue for a more active Jedi role, appealing to
others for support. Even from those Jedi—and their
untried students—who are struggling to maintain order
at home…}}

==Plot summary==
As another attempt to arrest [[Marn Hierogryph]] goes wrong, [[Zayne Carrick]] is rescued by a young Jedi named [[Darth Malak|Squint]]. Squint tells him that he and his master are heading to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] to fight in the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. He also attempts to recruit Zayne into their cause, but is unsuccessful, as Zayne wishes to fully devote himself to his Jedi training. As the issue closes, [[Lucien Draay]] and [[Q'Anilia]] discuss a necessary "change" in their plans.
